name: Webbhuset.Actor
comment:
# Actor

When a component is incorporated in a system it becomes an Actor. The actor module
implements the connections to the other components in the system.
In practice that means mapping and sending the component's out messages to other
actors in the system.

Here is an example of wrapping a login form component to an actor in a system.


This is the global model for the System:
```
module AppModel exposing (..)

type AppModel
    = LoginFormModel LoginForm.Model
    | OtherComponent ...
```

This is the global appMsg type for the System:
```
module AppMsg exposing (..)

type AppMsg
    = FormMsg LoginForm.MsgIn
    | AuthServiceMsg AuthService.MsgIn
    | OtherComponent ...

```

This is the login form actor:
```
module Actor.LoginForm exposing (..)

import Webbhuset.ActorSystem as System
import Webbhuset.Actor as Actor exposing (Actor)
import Component.LoginForm as LoginForm
import Component.AuthService as AuthService
import AppMsg exposing (AppMsg)
import AppModel exposing (AppModel)


actor : Actor LoginForm.Model AppModel AppMsg
actor =
    Actor.fromUI
        { wrapModel = AppModel.LoginFormModel
        , wrapMsg = AppMsg.FormMsg
        , mapIn = mapFormIn
        , mapOut = mapFormOut
        }
        LoginForm.component


mapFormIn : AppMsg -> Maybe LoginForm.MsgIn
mapFormIn appMsg =
    case appMsg of
        AppMsg.FormMsg formMsg ->
            Just formMsg

        _ ->
            Nothing


mapFormOut : PID -> LoginForm.MsgOut -> System.SysMsg name AppMsg
mapFormOut self formMsg =
    case formMsg of
        LoginForm.Submit user password ->
            AuthService.Login user password self
                |> AppMsg.AuthServiceMsg
                |> System.toAppMsg
                |> System.sendToSingleton AuthService

```

@docs PID

## Create Actors from Components

@docs fromUI, fromService, fromLayout

## Bootstrap

@docs Actor

Use these to send messages between actors in your system.

    , kill

## Bootstrap

Don't worry about these for now.

name: Webbhuset.Component
comment:
@docs PID

# Components

A component is a independent part in the system and has a single responsibility. They
can not import other components.

Components are like normal elm programs. They have their own
model, they can do commands and subscriptions. In addition to that
they also have out messages.

This means you will have two `Msg` types: `MsgIn` and `MsgOut`. MsgIn
is conceptually the same as your normal `Msg` type would be.

MsgOut is a way to "tell" the rest of the system that something happened in your component.

For Example, the msg types for a  login form component could look like this:

    type MsgIn
        = EmailFieldChanged String
        | PasswordFieldChanged String
        | SubmitbuttonClicked

    type MsgOut
        = FormWasSubmitted
            { email : String
            , password : String
            }

From the system's perspective, this is all you need to care about. This
is the public API for the component.

The normal output tuple from the `init` and `update` functions are replaced with
a 3-Tuple:

    ( Model, List MsgOut, Cmd MsgIn )

## UI Component

A UI-component is very similar in concept to a Browser.element program.

@docs UI

## Service Component

The service component does not have any view function. Remember `Platform.worker`?

@docs Service

## Layout Component

A layout component can render other components using their PID as a reference.
The difference comparing to a UI component is the view function.

@docs Layout

## Helpers for the output 3-Tuple

There is no native elm module for a Tuple with three arguments.

@docs mapFirst
    , mapSecond
    , mapThird
    , andThen
    , addOutMsg
    , addCmd
    , toCmd
    , toCmdWithDelay

## Helper for Queue

Sometimes you'd want to put messages in a queue. Maybe your
component is in a state where it can't process them at this point, eg. an InitState.

@docs Queue
    , emptyQueue
    , addToQueue
    , runQueue

aliases.name: Layout
aliases.comment:
Layout Component Type

The `view` function of a layout component:

    view : (MsgIn -> msg) -> Model -> (PID -> Html msg) -> Html msg
    view toSelf model renderPID =
        div
            []
            [ renderPID model.child
            , button [ onClick (toSelf ButtonWasClicked) ] [ text "Button!" ]
            ]

The `view` function has three arguments:

- `toSelf` is used to wrap all event-handlers from Html.Events
- `renderPID` is used to render other components.

As you can see, the output type of the `view` function is `Html msg`. This is
necessary to allow components to be composed. What would the return type be on
`renderPID` if they were not mapped to the same type?
aliases.type: { init : Webbhuset.Component.PID -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), update : msgIn -> model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), view : (msgIn -> msg) -> model -> (Webbhuset.Component.PID -> Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg, kill : model -> List.List msgOut, subs : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msgIn }

aliases.name: PID
aliases.comment: A PID is an identifier for a Process.

aliases.name: Service
aliases.type: { init : Webbhuset.Component.PID -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), update : msgIn -> model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), kill : model -> List.List msgOut, subs : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msgIn }

aliases.name: UI
aliases.comment:
UI Component Type

- **init**: Is called everytime the component is instantiated.
- **update**: When the component recieves a message.
- **view**: Is called when the app needs to re-render.
- **kill**: Is called when the component is no longer needed.
- **subs**: Normal Elm Subscriptions.
aliases.type: { init : Webbhuset.Component.PID -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), update : msgIn -> model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), view : model -> Html.Html msgIn, kill : model -> List.List msgOut, subs : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msgIn }

values.name: addCmd
values.comment:
Add a Cmd to the output 3-Tuple.

    ( model, [], Cmd.none )
        |> Component.addCmd cmd
values.type: Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg -> ( x, y, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ) -> ( x, y, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: addOutMsg
values.comment:
Add an out message to the output 3-Tuple.

    ( model, [], Cmd.none )
        |> Component.addOutMsg SomeOutMsg
values.type: msg -> ( x, List.List msg, y ) -> ( x, List.List msg, y )

values.name: addToQueue
values.comment:
Add a msg to the queue

    { model
        | queue = Component.addToQueue msgIn model.queue
    }
values.type: msgIn -> Webbhuset.Component.Queue msgIn -> Webbhuset.Component.Queue msgIn

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Run a series of updates on the model

The msgOut's and Cmd's will be composed using `System.batch` and 
`Cmd.batch`.

    ( model, [], Cmd.none )
        |> Component.andThen doSomethingWithModel
values.type: (model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn )) -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ) -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn )

values.name: emptyQueue
values.comment:
Create an Empty Queue

    { model
        | queue = Component.emptyQueue
    }
values.type: Webbhuset.Component.Queue msgIn

values.name: mapFirst
values.comment: Map the first argument (Model).
values.type: (input -> out) -> ( input, x, y ) -> ( out, x, y )

values.name: mapSecond
values.comment: Map the second argument (List MsgOut).
values.type: (input -> out) -> ( x, input, y ) -> ( x, out, y )

values.name: mapThird
values.comment: Map the third argument (Cmd).
values.type: (input -> out) -> ( x, y, input ) -> ( x, y, out )

values.name: runQueue
values.comment:
Run the `update` function on all messages in the queue
and compose all output.

    ( model, [], Cmd.none )
        |> Component.runQueue queue update
values.type: Webbhuset.Component.Queue msgIn -> (msgIn -> model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn )) -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ) -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn )

values.name: toCmd
values.comment: Convert a msg to Cmd.
values.type: msg -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: toCmdWithDelay
values.comment: Convert a msg to Cmd with a timeout in milliseconds.
values.type: Basics.Float -> msg -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

name: Webbhuset.Component.Sandbox
comment:
# Sandbox

The sandbox module is helpful when developing components. It lets you
run the component using `elm reactor` outside the system and define several test cases.

@docs SandboxProgram

@docs TestCase

## Sandbox UI Components

@docs ui

You can also use the ui sandbox on a Service component.
Just add a `view` function.

    { init = service.init
    , update = service.update
    , kill = service.kill
    , subs = service.subs
    , view = view
    }

    view : Model -> Html MsgIn
    view model =
        Html.text "Service Component"

aliases.name: SandboxProgram
aliases.comment: The Program type of your main function.

aliases.name: TestCase
aliases.comment:
A test case for the Component

Each test case contains a list of in-messages that will be sent to the component
when the sandbox is started.
aliases.type: { title : String.String, desc : String.String, init : List.List msgIn }

values.name: ui
values.comment:
Wrap a component in a sandbox application.

This will render each test case and log all messages.
Create a test file with a `main` function where you declare all
test cases.

    import Component.Form as Form
    import Webbhuset.Component.Sandbox as Sandbox exposing (SandboxProgram)

    main : SandboxProgram Form.Model Form.MsgIn
    main =
        Sandbox.ui
            Form.component
            "Form Component"
            [ Sandbox.TestCase
                "Empty form"
                "Submit-button should be disabled"
                [
                ]
            , Sandbox.TestCase
                "Form with Errors"
                "You should see an error message"
                [ Form.ErrorMsg "Error message"
                ]
            ]
values.type: { title : String.String, component : Webbhuset.Component.UI model msgIn msgOut, cases : List.List (Webbhuset.Component.Sandbox.TestCase msgIn), stringifyMsgIn : msgIn -> String.String, stringifyMsgOut : msgOut -> String.String } -> Webbhuset.Component.Sandbox.SandboxProgram model msgIn

aliases.name: PID
aliases.comment:
A PID is an identifier for a Process.

A process is an instance of an Actor / Component

values.name: null
values.comment: PID for testing purposes.
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.PID

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Stringify PID

This is useful when you need to make a unique string id, for example
to use as html ids.

The string is unique for the component instance.
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.PID -> String.String

name: Webbhuset.PID.Set
comment:
## Set of PID

Since PIDs are not comparable they can not be used with the Set in elm/core.

When using an event/observer pattern in the actor model it is useful
to gather PIDs that are observing an event in a Set.

@docs Set
    , PID
    , empty
    , insert
    , isEmpty
    , remove
    , toList

aliases.name: PID
aliases.comment:
A PID is an identifier for a Process.

A process is an instance of an Actor / Component

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty set
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set

values.name: insert
values.comment: Insert PID in a Set
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.Set.PID -> Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set -> Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Check if a Set is empty.
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set -> Basics.Bool

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove a PID from a Set
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.Set.PID -> Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set -> Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set

values.name: toList
values.comment: Get all pids as a List
values.type: Webbhuset.PID.Set.Set -> List.List Webbhuset.PID.Set.PID
